zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert II Stewart King of Scotland於1316年3月2日出生在蘇格蘭雷福福爾佩斯利時，他的父親Walter Stewart 6th High Steward of Scotland20歲，母親Majorie Bruce Princess of Scotland19歲。他於1347年11月22日與Elizabeth Mure of Rowallan在蘇格蘭艾斯爾凱爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1390年4月19日在蘇格蘭艾斯爾凱爾享年74歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭培爾笛卡爾 斯康斯康 普利奧利。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert II Stewart King of Scotland was born on 2 March 1316, in Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland, his father, Walter Stewart 6th High Steward of Scotland, was 20 and his mother, Majorie Bruce Princess of Scotland, was 19. He married Elizabeth Mure of Rowallan on 22 November 1347, in Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 6 daughters. He died on 19 April 1390, in Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland, at the age of 74, and was buried in Scone Priory, Old Scone, Perthshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert II Stewart King of Scotland est né le 2 mars 1316, à Paisley, Renfrewshire, Écosse, son père, Walter Stewart 6th High Steward of Scotland, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Majorie Bruce Princess of Scotland, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Mure of Rowallan le 22 novembre 1347, à Kyle, Ayrshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 6 filles. Il est décédé le 19 avril 1390, à Kyle, Ayrshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 74 ans, et a été enterré à Scone Priory, Old Scone, Perthshire, Écosse.

März 1316 in Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Walter Stewart 6th High Steward of Scotland 20 und seine Mutter Majorie Bruce Princess of Scotland 19. Er hat am 22. it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert II Stewart King of Scotland è nato il il 2 marzo 1316 nel Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland, il suo padre, Walter Stewart 6th High Steward of Scotland, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Majorie Bruce Princess of Scotland, aveva 19 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Mure of Rowallan il 22 novembre 1347, nel Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 19 aprile 1390, nel Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland, all'età di 74 anni, e fu sepolto a Scone Priory, Old Scone, Perthshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert II Stewart King of Scotlandが1316年3月2日にスコットランドレンフリューシュアイアペイスリーで生まれた時，父Walter Stewart 6th High Steward of Scotlandは20歳，母Majorie Bruce Princess of Scotlandは19歳であった。1347年11月22日にスコットランドエイルシュアイアクイルでElizabeth Mure of Rowallanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼は1390年4月19日にスコットランドエイルシュアイアクイルで74歳で死去し，スコットランドペルスシュアイアオルド スコンスコン プリオリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert II Stewart King of Scotland(이)가 1316년 3월 2일 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 파이슬리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Walter Stewart 6th High Steward of Scotland(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Majorie Bruce Princess of Scotland(은)는 19세였다. 그는 1347년 11월 22일 스컷랜드 에이르실르 키일에서 Elizabeth Mure of Rowallan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1390년 4월 19일 스컷랜드 에이르실르 키일에서 74세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 퍼스실르 드 스콘 스콘 플리오리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert II Stewart King of Scotland nasceu em 2 de março de 1316, em Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland, seu pai, Walter Stewart 6th High Steward of Scotland, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Majorie Bruce Princess of Scotland, tinha 19 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Mure of Rowallan em 22 de novembro de 1347, em Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 6 filhas. Ele faleceu em 19 de abril de 1390, em Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland, com 74 anos, e foi sepultado em Scone Priory, Old Scone, Perthshire, Scotland.

ru.Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Sc: пеисли эбби, пеисли, ренфрюшир, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert II Stewart King of Scotland родился 2 марта 1316 г. в местности пеисли, ренфрюшир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Walter Stewart 6th High Steward of Scotland было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Majorie Bruce Princess of Scotland, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Mure of Rowallan 22 ноября 1347 г. в местности кил, аиршир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 6 дочерей. Он умер 19 апреля 1390 г. в местности кил, аиршир, Шотландия в возрасте 74 лет и был похоронен в местности скоун приори, олд скоун, пиртшир, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert II Stewart King of Scotland nació el 2 de marzo de 1316, en Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland, su papá, Walter Stewart 6th High Steward of Scotland, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Majorie Bruce Princess of Scotland, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Mure of Rowallan el 22 de noviembre de 1347, en Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 19 de abril de 1390, en Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland, a la edad de 74 años, y fue sepultado en Scone Priory, Old Scone, Perthshire, Scotland.
